page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc5.4xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 architecture, utilizing an Intel Xeon 6149 processor running at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 22 MB of L3 cache, with hardware support for Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX and AVX-512F), AES cryptography, and Intel Hyper-Threading. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the ecs.hfc5.4xlarge demonstrates average multi-core processing capabilities and moderate memory throughput. Multi-core performance metrics, such as stress-ng multi-core CPU and Geekbench multi-core tests, place the instance in the middle 50% tier. Similarly, memory bandwidth tests show average performance, with read-only cached small block transfers reaching 804.1 GB/sec and uncached large block transfers at 73.32 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, falling into the bottom 25% across stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded gzip compression tests.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers dedicated compute resources with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, making it a viable option for workloads that require predictable CPU allocation without the overhead of high memory capacities. The lack of bundled local storage means storage costs are decoupled, which may influence overall deployment architecture.

The hardware profile and benchmark results make the ecs.hfc5.4xlarge suitable for multi-threaded workloads that can leverage its 16 dedicated cores. Appropriate use cases include static web serving, multi-threaded decompression tasks, Redis database operations, and software compilation. It is less suited for single-threaded applications or large-scale language model inference due to its weaker single-core performance and limited memory capacity.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc5.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated x86_64 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6149 processor running at 3.1 GHz. It is configured with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and contains no local storage or GPU accelerators. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates average multi-core performance and memory bandwidth, though single-core execution speeds are positioned in the lower tier. This resource profile offers a balanced, dedicated compute environment without the cost of excess memory. The ecs.hfc5.4xlarge is well-suited for multi-threaded workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, Redis operations, and medium-scale database tasks.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc5.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 32 GB RAM, and 3 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering average multi-core performance for web serving and database tasks.
